stories.title: Starmer Unveils £15bn Defence Investment Plan
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a £15bn uplift in defence spending, describing it as critical to protecting Britain. He warned the MoD must 'spend better' and said some road and energy projects will be cancelled to fund the rise.

stories.title: Guardian View: Defence Plan Cuts Civilian Investment While Deepening US Reliance
stories.summary: The Guardian's editorial board criticised Starmer's Defence Investment Plan for cutting civilian spending while increasing dependency on American military power, arguing it doesn't feel like 'national renewal'.

stories.title: Next Chancellor Will Face £5bn Black Hole in Defence Funding
stories.summary: Analysis reveals that the government's defence investment plan contains a significant funding gap, with the next chancellor expected to need to find an additional £5bn to cover the shortfall.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords & Legislation

stories.title: Eve Samson Appointed as 53rd Clerk of the House of Commons
stories.summary: Eve Samson, current Clerk of the Journals, has been appointed as the 53rd Clerk of the House of Commons following approval by His Majesty the King.

stories.title: House of Lords Debates National Security (State Threats) Bill
stories.summary: The House of Lords held all-stages debate on the National Security (State Threats) Bill on Tuesday 30 June, a key piece of legislation aimed at strengthening protections against foreign interference.

stories.title: Emergency Debate on Peter Mandelson Appointment as UK Ambassador to US
stories.summary: MPs debated the government's accountability regarding the appointment of Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to the United States in a House of Commons emergency debate.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy and internal politics

stories.title: Andy Burnham Wins Makerfield By-election, Paving Way for Leadership Challenge
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has won the Makerfield by-election, securing his return to Westminster and setting the stage for a formal leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

stories.title: Burnham Pledges to End Culture of Briefing Against Female Ministers
stories.summary: Labour leadership hopeful Andy Burnham told a meeting of the women's parliamentary Labour party that any staff who undermine female members of his team will be sacked.

stories.title: Labour Women Tell Burnham: Half of Government Should Be Female
stories.summary: Female MPs urged Andy Burnham to ensure Labour leads by example on gender equality should he become Prime Minister, calling for at least half of any future government to be women.

stories.title: Badenoch Takes Aim at Labour, Reform UK and Andy Burnham in Major Speech
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch delivered a high-profile speech on 29 June attacking Labour's defence funding plans and Andy Burnham's leadership bid, positioning the Tories as the credible opposition.

stories.title: Badenoch: Burnham Must Provide Right Level of Defence Funding
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch warned that if Labour leadership hopeful Andy Burnham cannot commit to adequate defence funding, he is unfit to lead the country.

stories.title: Tories Rule Out Deal with Plaid Cymru for Welsh Government Budget
stories.summary: Welsh Conservatives ruled out any deal with Plaid Cymru ministers ahead of the Welsh Government's first major budget test on 14 July, where the Plaid administration will need another party's support.

stories.title: Davey: Starmer's Defence Investment Plan Built on 'Fantasy Maths'
stories.summary: Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey criticised the government's Defence Investment Plan as built on 'fantasy maths', warning it leaves a massive funding gap that will need to be fixed by the next chancellor.

stories.title: Lib Dems: Government Has 'Dangerously Short-Changed' Armed Forces
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats accused the government of dangerously under-investing in the armed forces and called for urgent and substantial defence investment after years of Conservative negligence.

stories.title: Reform UK Chair of Welsh Environment Committee May 'Undermine Scrutiny'
stories.summary: Climate campaigners and a thinktank raised concerns that Reform UK's James Evans, appointed to chair the Welsh Senedd's environment committee, may undermine proper scrutiny given his history of criticising green energy projects.

stories.title: Unite Boss to Face Leadership Challenge Amid Concerns Over Reform UK Rise
stories.summary: Simon Dubbins announced he has secured the required nominations to challenge Unite union leader Sharon Graham, with concerns over Reform UK's growing influence in working-class communities cited as a key factor.

stories.title: Scottish Government Has 'No Idea' What Burnham's Devolution Plan Means
stories.summary: SNP ministers reacted with scepticism to Andy Burnham's proposal to redistribute power across the UK, saying the Scottish Government has received no details and is uncertain what the plan would mean for Scotland.

stories.title: Two New Scottish MPs Make Maiden Speeches After By-election Wins
stories.summary: The SNP's Lara Bird and the Conservatives' Douglas Lumsden delivered their maiden speeches in the House of Commons following their respective by-election victories.

stories.title: Defence Secretary Dan Jarvis Updates MPs on £15bn Military Spending Plan
stories.summary: Defence Secretary Dan Jarvis appeared before the House of Commons to update MPs on the Defence Investment Plan, describing the departmental budget cuts needed to fund it as 'difficult but necessary' choices.

stories.title: Draft Conversion Practices Bill Published for Consultation
stories.summary: The government published a draft Conversion Practices Bill that would make abusive conversion practices a criminal offence, alongside a final stage impact assessment for public scrutiny.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results & polls

stories.title: Andy Burnham Wins Makerfield By-election
stories.summary: Andy Burnham secured victory in the Makerfield by-election, returning to Westminster as an MP and immediately triggering speculation about a formal Labour leadership challenge against Keir Starmer.

stories.title: SNP's Lara Bird and Conservative Douglas Lumsden Win Scottish By-elections
stories.summary: Two Scottish by-elections produced wins for the SNP and the Conservative Party respectively, with both new MPs making their maiden speeches in the Commons shortly after their victories.

stories.title: House of Lords Debates National Security (State Threats) Bill
stories.summary: The House of Lords held an all-stages debate on the National Security (State Threats) Bill on 30 June, addressing measures to counter foreign state threats and espionage.

stories.title: Emergency Debate on Government Accountability Over Peter Mandelson Appointment
stories.summary: An emergency debate was held examining the government's accountability regarding Peter Mandelson's appointment as UK Ambassador to the United States, raising questions about the process and oversight.
